meta.program: Travel — Minimum Effective Dose (bodyweight + bands, multi-style)
meta.structure: A menu of session formats — pick by time and energy. Two hard strength days (Upper + Lower antagonist supersets) hold strength/size for weeks; Circuit and HIIT add density and conditioning; Knee Zero is the easy daily ATG maintenance. One repeating 4-week cycle.
meta.superset_convention: Exercises that share a letter group (A1 + A2) are an antagonist SUPERSET — done back to back, alternating, with short rest. The app groups them visually. A lone letter (D1) is a standalone exercise or a mechanical drop-set finisher.
meta.tempo_notation: 4 digits = eccentric/bottom-pause/concentric/top-pause seconds (e.g. 30X1). '-' = not a tempo movement (holds, sprints, timed intervals).
meta.styles: Antagonist supersets (Upper/Lower): pairs push with pull / quad with hamstring — ~half the time, same gains. Circuit/AMRAP (Full-Body): all moves back to back, repeat for rounds. Mechanical drop set (the D-finishers): hardest leverage to failure, drop to an easier variation, continue with no rest. HIIT/Tabata: 20s max effort / 10s rest x8 per move.
meta.progression: Leverage double progression within the rep range; when all sets hit the top, move to a harder VARIATION and reset. Intensity is non-negotiable: 1-2 near-failure sessions/week maintains strength and size for months (Spiering 2021; Sports Medicine 2024 minimal-dose review).
meta.sources: Supersets: Iversen/Weakley meta-analysis 2025 (Sports Med); Tabata 1996 / BodySpec protocol; mechanical drop sets (leverage) per calisthenics practice; Ben Patrick Knee Ability Zero.
meta.equipment: None required. A light band restores rows/face pulls/curls. Furniture: bed/chair = elevation, table = rows, doorframe = sissy assist, towel = leg curls + iso holds, stairs = Patrick step / calf raises.

Upper: Push — hardest version for 1-2 reps in reserve
Pull — slow, hard 1s squeeze at the top
Pull — external rotation at the end
Diamond Pushup or Band Triceps Ext
Finisher — pseudo-planche → feet-flat → knees, no rest. Each set = one drop to failure.
Lower: Quad — hold furniture for balance; elevate front foot to regress
Hamstring — towel under heels, bridge high, curl in slow
Quad/knee — doorframe assist; shins toward parallel
Hip/hamstring — 3s down; band under foot to load
Lower leg — full stretch at the bottom, stair edge
Lower leg (antagonist) — butt to wall, toes up, 2s hold
Finisher — pistol → split squat → bodyweight squat, no rest. Each set = one drop.
Circuit: Scale to incline/knees to keep moving
HIIT: Full extension up, chest to floor down
High Knees or Plank Shoulder Taps
Knee Zero: Anywhere — hallway, park, hill; knees over toes
Butt against wall, legs straight, toes up
Stair/step edge, full stretch at the bottom
Stair edge, knee over toes, slow lower
Bodyweight; elevate front foot on a step to regress
Rear foot on wall/couch, squeeze glute
